[PDF_PAGE 25 | score=6] Druid Kits -7 .action Bonuses and Penatties p. 18) is expressed as a bonus (+l, +2, etc.) or a penalty (-1, -2, etc.). When rolling 2d10 for encounter reactions (Table 59, DMG, p. 103), the DM must subtract the bonus or add the penalty-not the other way around. For instance, the druid Snap- dragon has a combined +7 reaction adjust- ment bonus for her high Charisma and her druid kit. The DM then subtracts 7 from the 2d10 encounter reaction roll to reflect the bonus, due to the way Table 59 in the DMG is designed. Kib and the Character Record Sheet To record a druid kit on your character record sheet (pgs. 125-127), take the follow- Using A druid kit works with the basic PH druid or with #ny o
[PDF_PAGE 42 | score=5] m middle class to poor to squalid) as become less hospitable. The druid can these penalties if he arranges with eone else (another druid or a ranger) to after the village in his absence. I Kabil's villagers also expect him to protect hem from serious harm. If he fails-or if no he sees him at least making an honest effort -the DM may reduce or eliminate his re- action bonus and benefits for as long as the illagers likely would feel resentful. Role- playing can win back a Village Druid's lost lrespeci; Kabil can regain his lost reaction bonus and benefits quickly by doing a great deed to benefit the village, or slowly by sim- ply completing his duties diligently for sev- leral months. I Wealth
[PDF_PAGE 100 | score=4] Wands and staves Wand of Shape Binding. Characters often use this item againsf druids. When hit by its multicolored beam (projected up to 80 feet), beings with the ability to shapechange or polymorph must save vs. wands at a -3 penalty. Victims who fail cannot voluntarily aIter shape for 2d10 turns. Attempts to shift shape using spells, magical items, or innate powers result in failure. A use of this rechargeable wand consumes one charge per 4 HD or levels of the subject. XP value: 800. Wanderer’s Staff. This resembles a stout oaken staff, which radiates magic and, in fact, functions as a quarterstaff+l. However, its pri- mary power is locomotion. If carried as a walking stick, users hiking 
[PDF_PAGE 23 | score=3] Agriculture: Expanded Rules The DM may use this expansion of the agri- culture proficiency when druid characters assist a small village facing tough times or if a PC takes up farming. These rules can figure the prosperity of an entire village if the DM groups area farms together and uses the pro- ficiency rating of the village leader or druid with Tables 1 and 2. Before applying the fol- lowing rules, the DM must decide how many people the farm in question is designed to support. A medieval farm needs a manager with the agriculture proficiency. At optimum level, a farm has one worker per every two people it supports. A farm with more workers may produce a slight surplus; if it has fewer work

Depending on the lord’s temper, an Adviser who has fallen into disfavor may face exile or worse until he makes amends. Wealth Options: 3d6x10 gp. [PDF_PAGE 39 | score=3] lruids in other branches have limits on their shapechanging powers. Weapon Proficiencies: Recommended- Secondary Skills: Hunter, groom. Nonweapon Proficiencies: Bonus- animal Recommended-(priest) spellcraft; (war- endurance, survival, tracking. uipment: The druid should spend her 1 allotment of gold pieces entirely on equipment, as she loses any unspent starting money in excess of 1 gp. Special Benefits: As a Shapeshifter, Rimi gains her branch’s shapechanging power at 1st level rather than at 7th level. However, until she reaches 7th level, the druid can assume only the form of natural creatures whose Hit Dice total no greater than half her level. (A lst- level Shapeshifter only assumes the
[PDF_PAGE 40 | score=3] Totemic Druid The Totemic Druid closely identifies with a particular species of mammal, reptile, or bird. While Vanier, a typical Totemic Druid, stops short of worshiping his totem animal, he believes that particular animal represents his spirit. The Totemic Druid picks a normal (real-world) wild mammal, reptile, or bird as his totem. This creature cannot be larger than a bear or smaller than a mouse. Some com- mon choices include the black bear, bobcat, eagle, owl, wolf, rattlesnake, and beaver. In addition, Vanier’s totem animal must corre- spond to his branch; if Vanier belongs to the desert druid branch, he can select as his totem only an animal that normally lives in the desert. Role: T
